page: Microsoft Azure D32a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.35 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 819 GB, and virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AES, SHA_NI, and BMI2, though it lacks AVX-512 support.

In benchmark evaluations, the D32a_v4 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, scoring 2707.98 in stress-ng tests. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier, though the PassMark memory score reaches the top 10% tier at 2984.77. For language model workloads, the server achieves a strong text generation speed of 323.62 tokens per second on small 135M models, but performance drops significantly to a weak 6.62 tokens per second for prompt processing on large 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32a_v4 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory or compute-optimized hardware. The primary tradeoff is the lack of AVX-512 and AMX instructions, which limits its utility for advanced vector mathematics and heavy deep learning workloads. It is best suited for standard enterprise applications, relational databases, web serving, and moderate software compilation tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D32a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine utilizing an AMD EPYC 7452 processor with 32 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core and includes 819 GB of local storage. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier PassMark memory scores, while multi-core operations and memory bandwidth sit in the average tier. The server lacks AVX-512 support, which limits its efficiency for advanced vector calculations. However, it delivers strong text generation speeds for small language models. Qualitatively, the D32a_v4 offers a cost-effective balance of compute and memory resources, making it highly suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads, web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 819 GB local storage, powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor for balanced enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32a_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 819 GB local storage, powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 CPU.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, substantial local storage, and strong single-core performance.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM and 819 GB of bundled local storage
Strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier PassMark memory scores
Lacks AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets, limiting advanced vector math
Best suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, databases, and web serving
